rationale: Empirical prober lens — every load-bearing claim verified in THIS tree by running commands. (1) WorkflowFiles() mirror is implementable: plugins/mindspec/embed.go uses //go:embed skills/*/SKILL.md + a fs.WalkDir SkillFiles() accessor; a //go:embed workflows/* + WorkflowFiles() mirror is a mechanical parallel. (2) installSkills chain confirmed: internal/setup/claude.go:159 -> internal/setup/skills.go:105, provenance-gated create/refresh/notice; RunCodex/RunCopilot install to .agents/skills/ and never touch it, so R8's Claude-target-only distribution holds. (3) grep -c workflows OWNERSHIP.yaml == 0 — the .claude/workflows/** claim is genuinely new (workflow claims .claude/skills/** but no broad .claude/**). (4) Classification reproduced: .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js -> isDocFile=false, isProcessArtifact=false = governable source; .claude is NOT in excludedFirstSegments; divergence.go:208-222 shows an unclaimed governable-source file trips adr-divergence-unowned. R9's same-diff-or-earlier premise is empirically sound. (5) mindspec validate spec 111 -> exit 0, only the expected lifecycle-binding WARN (no beads epic yet). All the spec's edit paths (plugins/mindspec, internal/setup, internal/validate, internal/panel) are already workflow-owned and claimed by no other domain — sole impacted domain = workflow holds. The spec is honest about the Claude Code workflow limits (no shell/fs, no output-schema, session-scoped resume) and designs around each; the adapter/authority boundary (never runs mindspec complete) is airtight. Findings below are impl/plan-time refinements, not spec blockers. Ready for approval.
findings: ADVISORY (self-disclosed in spec): the .js extension + ms-panel.js filename are an inference from the docs' 'JavaScript script' language, not a documented extension. Acceptance criteria hard-code .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js. At impl time the implementer must confirm the real Claude Code dynamic-workflow file extension/format; if it differs, the AC filenames and install target update accordingly. Not a spec-approval blocker (the risk is honestly named in Background).

ADVISORY (acceptance-criterion brittleness): AC line 104's `! grep -q 'mindspec complete' "$W"` will false-fail if the workflow carries any explanatory comment mentioning the string (and R5 heavily emphasizes 'never runs mindspec complete', so such a comment is likely). Plan phase should tighten this to an invocation-context match (e.g. exclude comment lines, or grep for an exec/agent-step pattern) so the check asserts 'never invokes' rather than 'never mentions'.

SIZING NOTE: R8's 'idempotent create/refresh, mirroring installSkills' implies replicating the provenance-gated shipped-snapshot machinery (previouslyShippedSkills/matchesShipped/cleanupRemovedSkills) for a flat .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js file — more than a trivial copy loop, since refresh-vs-user-modified discrimination needs its own snapshot history. Worth calling out for plan bead sizing; not a spec defect.
